title: Hazratbal Shrine
tagline: Hazratbal Shrine — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: The Hazratbal Shrine, also known as Dargah Hazratbal, is a Sufi dargah and mosque located in Hazratbal area of Srinagar, in the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India.
overview.description: The Hazratbal Shrine, also known as Dargah Hazratbal, is a Sufi dargah and mosque located in Hazratbal area of Srinagar, in the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The shrine houses the Moi-e-Muqqadas, a relic traditionally believed to be a hair strand of the Islamic Prophet Muhammad. The shrine is situated on the northern bank of Dal Lake in Srinagar and is regarded by many Muslims as the holiest Islamic religious sites in Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Hazratbal
topPlaces.description: Hazratbal is a notified area situated in the Srinagar district of Jammu and Kashmir. It is about 10 km (6.2 mi) from the Srinagar city center.

topPlaces.name: Naseem Bagh
topPlaces.description: Naseem Bagh is a Mughal garden built on the northwestern side of the Dal Lake, close to the city of Srinagar in Jammu and Kashmir, India. The garden is one of the oldest Mughal gardens in Kashmir, built by Mughal emperor Akbar in 1586.

topPlaces.name: Nigeen Lake
topPlaces.description: Nigeen Lake (alternatively spelled as Nageen Lake) is a lake located in Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is mildly eutrophic, a term that refers to a body of water rich in nutrients, which cause excessive growth of aquatic plants like algae, resulting in bacteria that consume nearly all the oxygen.

topPlaces.name: Lal Bazar
topPlaces.description: Lal Bazar is a locality in the municipal committee of Srinagar in the Indian administered union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It comes under the Zadibal constituency of the Srinagar Legislative Assembly.

topPlaces.name: Shalimar Bagh, Srinagar
topPlaces.description: Shalimar Bagh (Urdu pronunciation: [ʃɑːliːmɑːr bɑːɣ] ; Kashmiri pronunciation: [ʃaːlɨməːr baːɣ]) is a Mughal garden in Srinagar, Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir, located at the northeast of Dal Lake. It is also known as Shalimar Garden, Farah Baksh, and Faiz Baksh.

topPlaces.name: Badamwari Park
topPlaces.description: Badamwari Park or Badamvaer is a park in Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is known for the early bloom of almond flowers.

topPlaces.name: Dal Lake
topPlaces.description: Dal (Urdu pronunciation: [ɖəl] ; Kashmiri pronunciation: [ɖal]) is a freshwater lake in Srinagar, the summer capital of Jammu and Kashmir in Indian-administered Kashmir. It is an urban lake, the second largest lake in Jammu and Kashmir, and the most visited place in Srinagar by tourists and locals.

topPlaces.name: Awanta Bhawan
topPlaces.description: Awanta Bhawan, also spelled as Wontabhawan or Owanta Bhawan, is a historic locality adjacent to the Soura area of Srinagar, in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The area is associated with traditional water channels, historic religious sites, and old residential settlements in northern Srinagar, with historical associations with Rani Amritaprabha.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hazratbal Shrine?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hazratbal Shrine is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hazratbal Shrine?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (17 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (127 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hazratbal Shrine?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hazratbal Shrine at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hazratbal Shrine?
faq.a: Stays in Hazratbal Shrine start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hazratbal Shrine?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hazratbal, Naseem Bagh, Nigeen Lake, Lal Bazar, Shalimar Bagh, Srinagar.
